newsSince: No new material company-specific news since June 9. The NoScrubs autonomous laundry-delivery pilot was announced June 2-4 (before the June 9 base case) and was already digested in the prior #2 hold. The standing overhang remains the May 11 securities/dilution filing (Freedom Broker cut to Hold citing dilution/execution risk). Stock -5.7% since June 9 is flow-driven; commercialization thesis intact.

sources.label: Serve Robotics launches laundry vertical with NoScrubs (GlobeNewswire, Jun 2)
Serve Q1 2026 fleet revenue +578% / dilution concerns (Yahoo Finance)

verdicts.rankRationale: Deployed physical-AI fleet thesis intact; laundry/NoScrubs vertical was announced June 2-4 and already in the June 9 #2 hold. No new evidence since. -5.7% is flow; dilution from the May 11 offering and widening losses at a $0.56B cap still argue against pushing to #1. Hold at #2.
verdicts.bull: Deployed urban autonomous fleet (Ouster sensors, NVIDIA compute) with Q1 fleet revenue +578% and new non-food laundry vertical lifting utilization.
verdicts.neutral: Strong fleet momentum but tiny absolute revenue (~$26M FY26 guide); commercialization unproven at scale.
verdicts.bear: Heavy dilution from May 11 offering, widening losses, $0.56B cap, -45% YTD; execution and cash-burn risk.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Fleet scaling / new delivery verticals converting to recurring revenue
verdicts.primaryRisk: Further equity dilution and cash burn before unit economics turn

verdicts.rankRationale: Autonomous-delivery optionality unchanged since June 8 hold; the laundry pilot predates June 9. On the scaling lens this is a peripheral physical-AI optionality name, not core test-time-scaling. No new catalyst; hold at #17.
verdicts.bull: Optionality on physical-AI/autonomy scaling with a real deployed fleet.
verdicts.bear: Dilution overhang, -45% YTD, only tangential to compute-scaling thesis.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Dilution and burn; peripheral to scaling thesis
